reasoning: Direct US engagement in Iran in 2025 was limited to highly targeted airstrikes against nuclear facilities on June 22, 2025 (the '12 Days of War'). While these strikes occurred within the resolution window (beginning June 21), high-ranking US officials including Vice President JD Vance and Senator James Risch explicitly confirmed at the time that there were 'no boots on the ground.' Multiple sources describe the conflict as an 'air war' and 'airstrikes,' focusing on aerial territory which is specifically excluded by the resolution criteria. Following a ceasefire announced by President Trump on June 23, 2025, subsequent tensions remained 'below the radar,' consisting of explosions and technical sabotage rather than terrestrial ground combat by regular units. Given the exclusion of special operations forces and the administrative commitment against ground involvement, the probability of regular US military personnel conducting terrestrial combat operations in Iran is extremely low.
